_meta.source: Consensus of public Season 3 tier lists & win-rate trackers
_meta.tier_weights_note: Used in the Team tab to weight a counter's averaged score by each enemy's meta tier, so countering a meta-defining S/A pick is rewarded over countering a weak filler. 1.0 = no effect. Tune freely; set all to 1.0 to disable weighting globally.
_meta.disclaimer: Approximate community-consensus snapshot for a counter-pick app — NOT exact ladder stats. Competitive meta shifts every ~2-week patch and sources openly disagree (e.g. Reinhardt was rated S-tier by some win-rate sites and D-tier by editorial guides in the same week). Read S = currently dominant, A = strong/common, B = viable (default for unlisted heroes), C = niche, D = struggling. Refresh by re-running the research and replacing this file; the roster/kit data in heroes.json is far more stable and is verified separately via `npm run refresh-data`.

summary: Season 3 is a barrier-heavy poke meta in a tug-of-war with dive. Beam/anti-barrier damage that melts shields is on top: Torbjorn leads the whole roster (~55.6% win rate), with Symmetra (~54.1%) punishing the shield tanks (Sigma, Domina) and Zenyatta (~53.9%) anchoring supports. Sigma is the most-picked, most-reliable tank. Newest hero Shion is a strong backline diver. Vendetta has fallen off after survivability nerfs since her Season 1 dominance.

sigma.note: Most-picked tank with the best stat profile; barrier poke defines the meta.

torbjorn.note: Highest win rate in the game (~55.6%); turret + overload shred the shield meta.

symmetra.note: ~54.1% win rate; beam scaling melts Sigma/Domina barriers and tanks.

zenyatta.note: ~53.9% win rate; discord + volley output anchors the support line.

zarya.note: Beam shreds the barrier meta; bubbles answer dive.

junker-queen.note: Safe, self-sufficient brawl tank; anti-heal stays valuable.

wrecking-ball.note: Reliable dive/disruption pick that creates space cheaply.

sojourn.note: One of the strongest damage dealers; railgun bursts squishies and tanks.

reaper.note: Anti-tank shred thrives against the brawl/barrier frontlines.

pharah.note: Air angles pressure the grounded poke comps.

shion.note: High-mobility backline diver; strong in messy fights, season's newest threat.

emre.note: Versatile burst rifle + grenades; a Season 1 hero that stayed strong.

tracer.note: Dive staple that punishes immobile poke supports.

illari.note: Pylon sustain + burst rifle; strong ranged support value.

brigitte.note: Anti-dive utility keeps her relevant against Tracer/Shion.

wuyang.note: Flexible water support; healing + knockback utility everywhere in pro play.

lucio.note: Speed enables the brawl/dive tempo; near-permanent pick.

kiriko.note: Suzu cleanse + mobility; reliable in most comps.

juno.note: Mobility + amped output; strong flexible support.

reinhardt.note: Heavily contested — win-rate sites rate him near-top, editorial guides near-bottom because mobile heroes kite his shield. Treat as map/comp dependent.

domina.note: Middling (~50.7% win rate); strong barrier value but hard-countered by the beam DPS on top right now.

vendetta.note: Fell off after survivability nerfs; no longer the Season 1 powerhouse.

mizuki.note: Contested support — rated S by some lists, mid by others; situational.
